conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have three children - Alex is 7, Blake is 20, and Charlie is also 7. I'm wondering if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have three children - Alex (7), Blake (20), and Charlie (7).

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of them, I need to ask some questions.

Let me start with Blake, who is 20 years old. Is Blake currently in approved education or training? This would include things like:
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or equivalent)
- Approved training courses

If yes, when did Blake turn 20, and is Blake still enrolled in such education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in education or training. He actually works full-time at a local warehouse - he's been doing that for about a year now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since Blake is 20 and not in approved education or training, I'll need to note that.

Now, let me ask about your other two children, Alex and Charlie, who are both 7 years old. 

Do Alex and Charlie both live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. 

Are you responsible for Alex and Charlie? By this I mean, do you provide for their care and upbringing - things like feeding them, clothing them, and meeting their daily needs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I look after both of them - I provide all their care, feed them, clothe them, everything they need day to day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Are you currently living in the UK, and do Alex and Charlie also live in the UK with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester. Alex and Charlie both live here with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One more important question:

Are either Alex or Charlie currently in care (looked after by the local authority), or are they in hospital long-term?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in care or in hospital. They're both at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility:**

✅ **Alex (age 7)** - You ARE eligible for Child Benefit  
❌ **Blake (age 20)** - You are NOT eligible for Child Benefit  
✅ **Charlie (age 7)** - You ARE eligible for Child Benefit

**Explanation:**

- **For Alex and Charlie:** Since they are both 7 years old (under 16), live with you in the UK, and you're responsible for their care, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for them.

- **For Blake:** Unfortunately, Child Benefit is only available for young people aged 16-19 if they're in approved education or training. Since Blake is 20 years old and working full-time rather than being in education or training, you're not eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

You can proceed to claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 7 years old, which is under 16. Alex lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for Alex's care and upbringing. Alex is not in care or long-term hospital stay. According to the eligibility rules, you can get Child Benefit for a child under 16 who you're responsible for. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training - Blake works full-time at a warehouse. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can only be claimed for young people aged 16 to 19 if they are in approved education or training. Since Blake is 20 years old and not in qualifying education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 7 years old, which is under 16. Charlie lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for Charlie's care and upbringing. Charlie is not in care or long-term hospital stay. According to the eligibility rules, you can get Child Benefit for a child under 16 who you're responsible for. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children: Alex (7), Blake (20), and Charlie (7). The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) with all three children and is responsible for their care. Alex and Charlie, both being 7 years old (under 16), meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit as they live with the claimant who provides their care. Blake, however, at 20 years old and working full-time rather than being in approved education or training, does not meet the eligibility criteria. Child Benefit eligibility ends at age 20 or when a young person leaves approved education/training, whichever comes first. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie, but not for Blake.
